what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the cascade runner. It validates that a declared multi-step cascade can be saved through the existing build_action validator, discovered, persisted across reloads, and executed end-to-end by threading each step's output into the next step's input. It also checks fail-loud behavior for unknown cascades, unregistered roles, reduce-as-first-step, hardcoded models, and per-unit items failures, plus a source-invariant floor check that the cascade path does not call forbidden build/dispatch verbs.
observations: Defines a two-step proof cascade: MAP via run_items(repo_digest) followed by REDUCE via run_reduce(reduce_synth).
Uses SUITE.save_cascade, list_cascades, get_cascade, and run_cascade as the primary API surface.
Skips the live end-to-end run unless a resident brain is reachable at http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/models and the roles repo_digest and reduce_synth are registered.
Verifies save_cascade rejects a hardcoded model literal through the existing build_action/registry-is-truth path.
Verifies persistence by constructing a fresh ActionRegistry over the same file path and reading the saved cascade back.
Strips comments and blanks all string constants in the AST before scanning run_cascade/save_cascade source for forbidden verbs.
Monkeypatches _cog.run_role to raise on inputs containing 'POISON' and asserts the cascade raises naming the failed unit rather than silently shrinking the reduce input set.
Calls SUITE.refresh_self_description at the end so suite metadata reflects this new acceptance suite.
standouts: The floor check removes docstrings and string literals from source before scanning, so assertions naming forbidden verbs do not themselves trigger the scan.
Live end-to-end execution is gated by a real probe of a local :8000 model server, making the test conditionally green rather than recorded-only.
Failure injection via monkeypatching run_role tests the cascade's lane law: a failed unit must fail loudly, not be dropped from the reduce set.
concerns: Hardcodes http://127.0.0.1:8000 and specific role names repo_digest/reduce_synth, coupling the test to a particular local environment.
Globally monkeypatches _cog.run_role and restores it in a finally block; an unexpected exception could leave the patch in place.
Blanks every string constant in the AST for the floor scan, which may hide legitimate string-driven logic inside run_cascade or save_cascade.
Uses a /tmp store path keyed by pid, which is isolated but not durable across test restarts.
notes: An acceptance test that proves a saved cascade can be validated, persisted, and run end-to-end with threaded outputs, while enforcing strict fail-loud validation and a no-build floor.
questions: What exact fields does SUITE.run_cascade return, and are final_address and final_output always present?
Is the monkeypatched per-unit failure mode meant to be reproduced by production code, or is it only a test artifact?
How does build_action validate models when the resident brain's id is not returned by available_models()?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it exercises save, run, indexing, fail-loud, and floor behavior for the cascade runner.
apparent_themes: End-to-end pipeline execution with persisted intermediate outputs
Source-invariant floor preventing code-build verbs in the cascade path
intention_signals: To demonstrate that the cascade runner is a thin, net-new orchestration layer atop existing engine primitives, not a parallel registry or engine, and that it remains safe and observable under validation and failure.
novelty: The test injects a simulated per-unit model failure into an items step to verify the cascade fails loudly and names the failed unit instead of silently reducing the input set passed to the reduce step.
contribution: An executable acceptance contract for cascade save/run semantics, including validation gate behavior, end-to-end output addressing, per-step run indexing, and failure-mode assertions that a fused system would keep as living documentation.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for a saved multi-step cascade runner that validates persistence, end-to-end execution, per-step indexing, fail-loud error handling, and a no-build floor.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the cascade runner reuses existing engine primitives to execute declared pipelines with threaded outputs and strict validation/failure semantics.

symbols.does: Counts passing assertions and raises AssertionError with a label when a condition is false.
symbols.description: The suite's central assertion helper. It increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok line for each successful check; on failure it raises with the provided label, driving the suite's pass/fail reporting.

symbols.does: Probes http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/models to decide whether live cascade execution should run.
symbols.description: Returns True if a resident model server responds within 4 seconds on localhost:8000. The result gates the live end-to-end cascade leg, causing it to be skipped when no brain is present.

symbols.does: Returns a function's source with comments and all string literals removed for forbidden-verb scanning.
symbols.description: Parses a function's source with ast, drops comment lines, blanks every string constant in the AST, and unparses the result. This lets the floor check inspect only executable code, avoiding false positives from docstrings that merely name forbidden verbs.

symbols.does: Test wrapper around _cog.run_role that raises RuntimeError when the input contains 'POISON'.
symbols.description: Injects a simulated per-unit model failure so the test can verify that a cascade items step fails loudly and reports the failed unit, rather than silently dropping it and producing a shorter address list for the reduce step.
